input.prompt:
I'm coming from ASP.NET Core and I registered my DbContext with
AddScoped in my MAUI app. My data seems to persist across page
navigations when it shouldn't. What's going on?

I'm coming from ASP.NET Core and I registered my DbContext with

input.initialPrompt:
I'm coming from ASP.NET Core and I registered my DbContext with
AddScoped in my MAUI app. My data seems to persist across page
navigations when it shouldn't. What's going on?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common pitfall when migrating from ASP.NET Core to .NET MAUI regarding dependency injection scoping — specifically why `AddScoped` behaves differently in MAUI than in ASP.NET Core.

This is a conceptual question I can answer directly without needing to search any codebase.

In ASP.NET Core, `AddScoped` creates a service instance per HTTP request — each request gets its own scope, so the DbContext is fresh for each request.

In .NET MAUI, there is no concept of an HTTP request. The DI container creates a single scope for the entire application lifetime by default. So `AddScoped` effectively behaves like `AddSingleton` — your DbContext lives for the entire app lifetime, which is why data persists across page navigations.


To fix this, I can either switch to `AddTransient` to get a fresh DbContext instance on each request, or manually manage scopes using `IServiceScopeFactory` for more granular control.
